text: Good evening. Before the day slips away — let's look at it together. This will take about five minutes. No scores. No grades. Just a quiet mirror.
If today were a weather report, what would it be?
options: Sunny — things went my way
Cloudy — mixed, uncertain
Stormy — hard and draining
Foggy — I'm not sure what happened
answer=Sunny — things went my way|Cloudy — mixed, uncertain:A1_Q1_HIGH;answer=Stormy — hard and draining|Foggy — I'm not sure what happened:A1_Q1_LOW

text: You described today as '{A1_OPEN.answer}'. When something went well, what do you think made it happen?
options: I had prepared and it showed
I adjusted when things shifted
Someone helped me at the right moment
I got lucky — hard to say why

text: You described today as '{A1_OPEN.answer}'. When things got difficult, where did your mind go first?
options: I looked for what I could still control
I felt stuck waiting for something to change
I blamed myself — probably too harshly
I wondered why this keeps happening to me
answer=I had prepared and it showed|I adjusted when things shifted:A1_Q2_AGENCY;answer=Someone helped me at the right moment|I got lucky — hard to say why:A1_Q2_LUCK

answer=I looked for what I could still control:A1_Q2_AGENCY;answer=I felt stuck waiting for something to change|I blamed myself — probably too harshly|I wondered why this keeps happening to me:A1_Q2_STUCK

text: Think of one moment today where things didn't go as planned. What did you do?
options: I found a different path to the same goal
I accepted it and moved on
I asked for help and kept moving
I tried to push through, even if it cost me

text: When things got hard today, was there a moment — even a small one — where you had a choice?
options: Yes, and I made a call — not sure if it was right
Yes, but I didn't act on it
I'm not sure I saw any choice at the time
Looking back now, I think there was one

text: Even on days when things go your way, there's usually something *you* did that helped. What was yours today?
options: I showed up with energy and focus
I kept communication clear
I stayed patient when I could have snapped
Honestly, I'm not sure — it just happened
answer=I found a different path to the same goal|I accepted it and moved on|I asked for help and kept moving:A1_R_INT;answer=I tried to push through, even if it cost me:A1_R_INT

answer=Yes, and I made a call — not sure if it was right|Yes, but I didn't act on it|Looking back now, I think there was one:A1_R_EXT_GROW;answer=I'm not sure I saw any choice at the time:A1_R_EXT_STUCK
answer=I showed up with energy and focus|I kept communication clear|I stayed patient when I could have snapped:A1_R_INT;answer=Honestly, I'm not sure — it just happened:A1_R_EXT_GROW

text: You stayed in the driver's seat today — not perfectly, but consciously. That's the whole thing: not that nothing went wrong, but that you kept looking for your next move. That's agency. Not a personality trait. A practice.

A tough day pulls your gaze outward — to what went wrong, who let you down, what you couldn't control. That's human. But you just noticed there was a choice somewhere in there. That noticing? That's the start of something.

Some days feel entirely out of your hands. And sometimes they genuinely are. But here's a small question to sleep on: was there one moment — even a two-second one — where your response was yours to choose? You don't have to answer now.
Now let's shift from how you *handled* the day — to what you *gave* to it.
Think of one interaction you had today. In that moment, which of these felt more true?
options: I was thinking about what I could offer
I was thinking about what I would get
I was thinking about getting through it
I wasn't really thinking — I was on autopilot

answer=I was thinking about what I could offer:A2_Q1_CONTRIB;answer=I was thinking about what I would get|I wasn't really thinking — I was on autopilot:A2_Q1_ENT;answer=I was thinking about getting through it:A2_Q1_NEUTRAL

text: You said you were thinking about what you could offer. Did you actually do something beyond what was expected of you today?
options: Yes — I helped with something that wasn't mine to do
Yes — I put in extra effort on something that mattered
Sort of — I tried, but it might not have landed
Not today, but I wanted to

text: Think honestly: was there a moment today where you felt you deserved more than you received — more credit, more help, more recognition?
options: Yes, and it bothered me for a while
Yes, but I let it go quickly
Briefly, but it passed
No — I didn't really think about it

text: When you were in 'just get through it' mode — who else was in the room with you (literally or figuratively)?
options: A colleague who was also struggling
My manager or someone I report to
A customer or external person
I was mostly alone with the task

answer=Yes — I helped with something that wasn't mine to do|Yes — I put in extra effort on something that mattered:A2_Q2_CONTRIB_DEEP;answer=Sort of — I tried, but it might not have landed|Not today, but I wanted to:A2_Q2_CONTRIB_SOFT
answer=Yes, and it bothered me for a while|Yes, but I let it go quickly:A2_Q2_ENT;answer=Briefly, but it passed|No — I didn't really think about it:A2_Q2_CONTRIB_SOFT
answer=A colleague who was also struggling|A customer or external person:A2_Q2_CONTRIB_DEEP;answer=My manager or someone I report to|I was mostly alone with the task:A2_Q2_CONTRIB_SOFT

text: When you gave something extra today, what was driving it?
options: I genuinely wanted to help
I felt it was the right thing to do
I hoped it would be noticed
A bit of all of these — I'm not sure

text: Is there something you could have done today — for a colleague, a customer, the team — that you didn't do? What got in the way?
options: I was too stretched — genuinely no bandwidth
I noticed but chose not to act
I didn't notice until just now
I did act — maybe I'm being too hard on myself

text: The feeling of not getting enough recognition is real. But let's flip it: did someone else give more than they received today?
options: Yes — a colleague clearly carried more than their share
Probably, but I didn't really notice at the time
Maybe — I wasn't paying attention that way
No, I think it was roughly fair
answer=I genuinely wanted to help|I felt it was the right thing to do:A2_R_CONTRIB;answer=I hoped it would be noticed|A bit of all of these — I'm not sure:A2_R_MIXED
answer=I was too stretched — genuinely no bandwidth|I did act — maybe I'm being too hard on myself:A2_R_CONTRIB;answer=I noticed but chose not to act|I didn't notice until just now:A2_R_MIXED

answer=Yes — a colleague clearly carried more than their share|Probably, but I didn't really notice at the time:A2_R_ENT_GROW;answer=Maybe — I wasn't paying attention that way|No, I think it was roughly fair:A2_R_ENT

text: You gave something today without keeping score. That's not a small thing. Organizational citizenship — the discretionary effort that no job description captures — is what makes a team more than a group of individuals.

Most of us give for mixed reasons — genuine care, self-interest, habit, obligation. That's honest. The question isn't purity of motive. It's whether, on balance, you moved toward giving or taking. Today sounds like a lean toward giving.

You noticed someone else's contribution today. That's a shift. Entitlement is invisible to the person feeling it — it's a lens, not a feeling. The moment you start seeing what others are putting in, the lens is already changing.

It's worth noticing: you spent some energy today tracking what you deserved. Everyone does this sometimes. The question for tomorrow is whether there's a moment where you lead with giving before you start counting.
One last shift. From what you *gave* — to who you *saw*.
When you think about today's most significant moment, whose experience comes to mind — besides your own?
options: No one in particular — it was really about me
A specific colleague who was also in it
The team or group as a whole
Someone outside — a customer, partner, or end user

answer=No one in particular — it was really about me:A3_Q1_SELF;answer=A specific colleague who was also in it:A3_Q1_COLLEAGUE;answer=The team or group as a whole|Someone outside — a customer, partner, or end user:A3_Q1_WIDE

text: Being absorbed in your own experience isn't wrong — sometimes the work demands it. But just for a moment: was there anyone around you today who might have had a harder time than they showed?
options: Yes, now that I think about it — someone was quieter than usual
Maybe — I didn't look that closely
I don't think so — everyone seemed fine
I genuinely had no bandwidth to notice

text: You thought of a specific person. Did you do anything with that awareness today — or did you notice and move on?
options: I checked in with them or helped in some way
I noticed but didn't act — didn't want to overstep
I noticed but was too busy
I noticed after the fact, too late to do anything

text: When you think about the team or customer you mentioned — was your work today connected to something that matters to them?
options: Yes — I can draw a direct line from my work to their outcome
I think so, but the connection is fuzzy
Probably, but I wasn't thinking about it that way today
Not directly — but it's there in the bigger picture

answer=Yes, now that I think about it — someone was quieter than usual:A3_Q2_SELF_GROW;answer=Maybe — I didn't look that closely|I genuinely had no bandwidth to notice:A3_Q2_SELF_GROW;answer=I don't think so — everyone seemed fine:A3_Q2_SELF

answer=I checked in with them or helped in some way:A3_Q2_ALTRO;answer=I noticed but didn't act — didn't want to overstep|I noticed but was too busy|I noticed after the fact, too late to do anything:A3_Q2_COLLEAGUE_PASSIVE

answer=Yes — I can draw a direct line from my work to their outcome:A3_Q2_ALTRO;answer=I think so, but the connection is fuzzy|Probably, but I wasn't thinking about it that way today|Not directly — but it's there in the bigger picture:A3_Q2_WIDE_SOFT

text: What does success look like for you at work — in one sentence?
options: I do my job well and advance my career
My team hits its goals and I'm part of that
The people I work for get what they need
Something larger — leaving things better than I found them

text: If you had noticed that person earlier, what might you have done?
options: Just asked how they were doing
Helped with whatever was weighing on them
Probably nothing — I'm not sure it was my place
Shared what I was dealing with too — sometimes that helps

text: When you act beyond your own immediate interests — what makes it worth it for you?
options: The relationship — it builds something real
The meaning — it connects my work to something bigger
The team — we all move forward together
It's just what you do — I don't analyse it

text: You noticed something but didn't act. What usually stops you from reaching out to a colleague who's struggling?
options: Fear of making it worse or overstepping
Time — I barely had enough for my own work
I assumed someone else would step in
Habit — I tend to keep to myself

text: If the person or group you mentioned could see your work today, what would you want them to feel about it?
options: That someone was thinking about them, not just the task
That the work was done with care
That it moved things forward for them

text: There's nothing wrong with showing up for yourself. Everyone needs to. But Maslow — in his later, lesser-known work — noticed that the people who reported the deepest satisfaction weren't the ones who got the most. They were the ones who found their work connected to something beyond themselves. Worth a thought.

You're starting to see past your own frame. That's perspective-taking — not sympathy, but the harder thing: genuinely imagining another person's experience from the inside. It changes what you notice. It changes what you do next.

You're operating with a wide radius today — your work landed somewhere beyond just yourself. That's what Maslow called self-transcendence: not self-sacrifice, but the recognition that meaning comes from contributing to something that outlasts the moment. You did that today.
See you tomorrow.
Summary text templates by axis and dominant

axis1.summary_internal: You kept looking for your next move, even when things were outside your control. Agency isn't the absence of difficulty — it's the habit of asking 'what can I do from here?'
axis1.summary_external: A hard day pulled your attention to what you couldn't control. That's honest. The question to carry is: where, in all of that, was the moment that was yours?

axis2.summary_contribution: You gave something today. Not perfectly, not without mixed motives — but you moved toward others more than toward the ledger.
axis2.summary_entitlement: You noticed today through the lens of what you deserved. Everyone does this. The question for tomorrow: can you lead with one action before you start counting?

axis3.dominant_self: inward — your own experience was the primary frame
axis3.dominant_other: outward — you saw at least one other person clearly
axis3.dominant_altrocentric: wide — beyond yourself to the team, customer, or something larger
axis3.summary_self: Self-focus is sometimes necessary. But the research is clear: the people who report the most meaning at work are those whose concern extends past themselves.
axis3.summary_other: You saw someone else today. That's not a small thing. Perspective-taking is a skill — the more you practice it, the more naturally it comes.
axis3.summary_altrocentric: You connected your work to something beyond yourself today. That's the thing Maslow spent his later career trying to name: self-transcendence. You touched it.
